## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(خ›, E_خ›)`** â€” finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal â€” not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, â„¤^d, â€¦) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the Marshallâ€“Liebâ€“Mattis theorem, Miyao 2021
آ§3, â€¦) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `خ› : Type*` together with `[Fintype خ›]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `â„¤` or `â„¤^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : خ› â†’ خ› â†’ â„‚` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and Collatzâ€“Wielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank â„‚ â‰¤ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D â‰¥ 0` version because single-ion `آ±2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D â‰¥ 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **خ»=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|خ›|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `خ» = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `خ» = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 â‰¤ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `خ» = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `خ» â‰¥ 1`, `D â‰¤ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M âˆ© Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M âˆ© Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t âˆˆ Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the آ±2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 â‰¤ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < خ» < 1`, `D â‰¥ 0`; `خ» = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < خ»`, `D â‰¤ 0`. |

So for a `إœآ³=آ½`, `Nج‚=Ne` ground state (`Nج‚_â†“=(Neâˆ’1)/2`), the top `خ©=(إœâپ؛)^((Neâˆ’1)/2)خ¦` satisfies `إœâپ؛خ©=0` â€” a highest weight. **E3b PR4 (`TJHighestWeight.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_raised_highestWeight` combines the tower tracking + termination â€” `خ¦` with `إœآ³خ¦=آ½خ¦`, `Nج‚_â†“خ¦=mخ¦`, `ؤ¤_tJخ¦=خ¼خ¦` âں¹ `خ©=(إœâپ؛)^m خ¦` satisfies `إœâپ؛خ©=0`, `إœآ³خ©=(m+آ½)خ©`, `ؤ¤_tJخ©=خ¼خ©` (for `m=(Neâˆ’1)/2`, `إœآ³خ©=(Ne/2)خ©`, the maximal-spin highest weight); **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `أ‚` is `â‰¥0` iff all eigenvalues `â‰¥0`), **A.5** (`أ‚,Bج‚â‰¥0 â‡’ أ‚+Bج‚â‰¥0`), **A.6** (`Bج‚â€ Bج‚â‰¥0`, and conversely every `أ‚â‰¥0` is `ؤˆآ²` for a *unique* PSD square root `ؤˆ=âˆڑأ‚` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/PosSemidef/Basics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `أ‚â‰¤Bج‚` â‡’ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `أ‚.eigenvaluesâ‚€ i â‰¤ Bج‚.eigenvaluesâ‚€ i`) **now proved (axiom-free)** via the Courantâ€“Fischer block/pigeonhole argument (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/CourantFischer.lean`: spectral Rayleigh-sum `reâں¨x,Txâں©=âˆ‘خ»لµ¢â€–âں¨bلµ¢,xâں©â€–آ²` + block bounds + the top-`(i+1)`/bottom-`(cardâˆ’i)` eigenspaces meeting by finrank pigeonhole; `EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`أ‚â‰¤Bج‚ â‡’ Tr e^أ‚ â‰¤ Tr e^Bج‚`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^أ‚ = خ£ e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `ؤ¤=خ£ؤ¥â±¼`, `ؤ¥â±¼â‰¥خµâ±¼`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate â‡’ ground state at `خ£خµâ±¼`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`أ‚â‰¥0 âˆ§ âں¨خ¦|أ‚|خ¦âں©=0 â‡’ أ‚خ¦=0`; `أ‚=Bج‚â€ Bج‚ â‡’ Bج‚خ¦=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `ؤ´آ²خ¦=0 â‡’ ؤ´âپ½لµ…âپ¾خ¦=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`ؤ¤_v=ؤ¤â‚€+vVج‚`, `Vج‚â‰¥0`: `vâ†‘âˆ‍` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `خ¦` satisfy the effective Schrأ¶dinger eq `Pج‚â‚€ؤ¤â‚€خ¦=Eخ¦` on `Hâ‚€=ker Vج‚`) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto: the paired eigenvalue relation gives `vآ·âں¨خ¦_v|Vج‚|خ¦_vâں© = E_vâ€–خ¦_vâ€–آ²âˆ’âں¨خ¦_v|ؤ¤â‚€|خ¦_vâں©`, the converging right side times `vâپ»آ¹â†’0` kills the limiting quadratic form, Lemma A.11 kills `Vج‚خ¦`; pairing with `دˆ âˆˆ ker Vج‚` removes the `vVج‚` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **آ§A.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) â€” operator identity `ؤ´âپ»ؤ´âپ؛=ؤ´آ²âˆ’ؤ´آ³(ؤ´آ³+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`âˆ’Jâ‰¤M<J`, `خ¦â‰ 0` â‡’ `ؤ´âپ؛خ¦â‰ 0`) + raising **membership** (`ؤ´âپ؛خ¦âˆˆH_{J,M+1}`, via `[ؤ´آ³,ؤ´âپ؛]=ؤ´âپ؛` + `[ؤ´آ²,ؤ´âپ؛]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`ؤ´âپ؛ؤ´âپ»=ؤ´آ²âˆ’ؤ´آ³(ؤ´آ³âˆ’1)` eq A.3.8, `ؤ´âپ»خ¦â‰ 0` for `âˆ’J<Mâ‰¤J`, `ؤ´âپ»خ¦âˆˆH_{J,Mâˆ’1}`) **proved** â€” A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `â€–ؤ´آ±خ¦â€–آ²={J(J+1)âˆ’M(Mآ±1)}â€–خ¦â€–آ²` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ؤ´^خ±) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`âˆ’Jâ‰¤Mâ‰¤J`, i.e. `Jâˆ’M,J+Mâ‰¥0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `ؤ´âپ؛`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `Jâˆ’M âˆˆ â„¤â‰¥0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(ؤ´آ¹,âˆ’ؤ´آ²,âˆ’ؤ´آ³)` gives `J+M âˆˆ â„¤â‰¥0`, so `2J=(Jâˆ’M)+(J+M)âˆˆâ„¤â‰¥0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `ؤ¤` (`[ؤ¤,ؤ´لµ…]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,Jâˆ’k}` (`kâ‰¤2J`) â€” `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is â‰¥`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `ؤ¤` has an eigenstate with `ؤ´آ³=0` or `ؤ´آ³=1/2` â€” `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `ؤ´آ²` being PSD and commuting with `ؤ´آ³`/`ؤ¤`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (Perronâ€“Frobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(cآ·1âˆ’M)` irreducible (off-diag `â‰¤0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector â€” `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's Collatzâ€“Wielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D Vâ€ `, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d â‰¥ 0`) â€” **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `Aل´´A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `خ»_i â‰¥ 0`, `d_i = âˆڑخ»_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iâپ»آ¹آ·A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vل´´`, `C = V D Vل´´` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear âˆ—-automorphism of `M(H)` âں¹ `خ“(أ‚)=أ›â€ أ‚أ›`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear âں¹ `أ›â€ أ‚ج„أ›`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P Pâ€²]` âں¹ (anti)unitary `Vج‚`) â€” documented axioms over `Matrix D D â„‚` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / Banachâ€“Alaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-âˆ—-continuous `د† : WeakDual â„‚ A` with `د† 1 = 1`, `0 â‰¤ د†(aâ€ a)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-âˆ— compact, documented axiom) â€” `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `خ´=[ؤ¤,آ·]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 â‰¤ د‰(aâ€ آ·خ´a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`خ³>0`, `د‰(aâ€ خ´a) â‰¥ خ³د‰(aâ€ a)` on `د‰(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state âں؛ `د‰(ؤ¤_L)` least over states agreeing outside خ›_L, documented axiom) â€” `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` â€” every state `دپ` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_دپ, د€_دپ, خ©_دپ)` (`âˆ—`-representation `د€ : A â†’â‹†â‚گ[â„‚] (Hâ†’L H)` + cyclic `خ©`) with `دپ(أ‚)=âں¨خ©,د€(أ‚)خ©âں©` and `{د€(أ‚)خ©}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **آ§A.6â€“آ§A.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete â†’ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1â€“A.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (آ§11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** â€” the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `â„‌`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `vâپ»آ¹`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4â€“A.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7â€“A.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via Courantâ€“Fischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9â€“A.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13â€“A.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (Perronâ€“Frobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's Collatzâ€“Wielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19â€“A.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** â€” the
  operator-algebraic **A.21â€“A.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, Banachâ€“Alaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* â€” they record exactly what Tasaki proves â€” but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. The heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic results (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-âˆ—
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**. Accordingly the project's policy is to
**axiomatize only the appendix results that Tasaki's formalized main development
actually uses**, to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the
tools, and otherwise to leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in a
large bespoke operator-algebra build whose natural home is elsewhere. The
`#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes the precise dependency
on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, آ§2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
آ§2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(دƒ^خ±)آ² = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

### Zâ‚‚ أ— Zâ‚‚ representation (Tasaki آ§2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The Zâ‚‚ أ— Zâ‚‚ structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

### Spin-`S` operators (general S â‰¥ 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : â„•`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, آ§2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) â„‚`, with `N = 2S : â„•` (so `N = 1` â‡” `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` â‡” `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki آ§2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `إœ^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, â€¦, -N/2)` for `N : â„•` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `âˆڑ(kآ·(N âˆ’ k + 1))` (resp. `âˆڑ((N âˆ’ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `إœ^(1) := (إœ^+ + إœ^-) / 2`, `إœ^(2) := (إœ^+ âˆ’ إœ^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `إœ^+` annihilates `\|Nâں©` (highest weight); `إœ^-` annihilates `\|0âں©` (lowest weight) â€” the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[إœ^{(3)}, إœ^+] = إœ^+` and `[إœ^{(3)}, إœ^-] = -إœ^-`: `إœ^آ±` shift the magnetic quantum number by `آ±1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (خ²-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(إœ^+)ل´´ = إœ^-`, `(إœ^-)ل´´ = إœ^+`, and Hermiticity of `إœ^{(خ±)}` for `خ± âˆˆ {1, 2, 3}` (خ²-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `إœ^{(1)}` and `إœ^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(خ´_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(إœ^{(3)} âˆ’ خ»_j â€¢ 1) آ· P_k = (خ»_k âˆ’ خ»_j) آ· P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (خ²-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `âˆڈ_{j â‰  k} (إœ^{(3)} âˆ’ خ»_j â€¢ 1) / (خ»_k âˆ’ خ»_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `إœ^{(3)} آ· P_k = خ»_k آ· P_k` (where `خ»_k = (N : â„‚)/2 âˆ’ k`), with the symmetric `P_k آ· إœ^{(3)} = خ»_k آ· P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (خ²-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A آ· P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k آ· A`). Specialised to `إœ^آ±` produces off-diagonal matrix units (خ²-6 of Issue #458) â€” the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `إœ^+ آ· P_{k+1} آ· إœ^- = (k + 1)(N âˆ’ k) آ· P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `إœ^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1ج‚, إœ^{(خ±)}}` (خ²-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `إœ^+ آ· P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `إœ^- آ· P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `إœ^آ±` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (خ²-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `خ£_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (خ²-6, خ²-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(â„‚)` (خ²-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `إœ^+ آ· P_{i+1} = âˆڑ((i+1)(Nâˆ’i)) آ· E_{i, i+1}` and `إœ^- آ· P_i = âˆڑ((Nâˆ’i)(i+1)) آ· E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (خ²-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `إœ^آ±` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(إœ^{(3)})آ² = diag((N/2 âˆ’ i)آ²)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(إœ^{(1)})آ² + (إœ^{(2)})آ² + (إœ^{(3)})آ² = (N(N+2)/4) آ· 1` for general spin (خ²-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `إœ^+ آ· إœ^- = diag((i + 1)(N âˆ’ i))`. The product is diagonal because `إœ^+[i, l] آ· إœ^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `إœ^- آ· إœ^+` and `(إœ^{(3)})آ²`, this assembles the Casimir identity (خ²-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `إœ^- آ· إœ^+ = diag(i آ· (N âˆ’ i + 1))`. Symmetric to خ²-12 with `إœ^آ±` swapped (خ²-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(إœ^{(1)})آ² + (إœ^{(2)})آ² + (إœ^{(3)})آ² = (N(N+2)/4) آ· 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) آ· 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(إœ^{(1)})آ² + (إœ^{(2)})آ² = (1/2)(إœ^+ آ· إœ^- + إœ^- آ· إœ^+)` is proved using `module` (خ²-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `إœ^{(3)} آ· \|kâں© = (N/2 âˆ’ k) آ· \|kâں©`, where `\|kâں© := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) â†’ â„‚)` (خ²-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `إœ^+ آ· \|kâں© = âˆڑ(k(N âˆ’ k + 1)) آ· \|k âˆ’ 1âں©` for `k â‰¥ 1`, and `إœ^- آ· \|kâں© = âˆڑ((N âˆ’ k)(k + 1)) آ· \|k + 1âں©` for `k â‰¤ N âˆ’ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (خ²-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(إœ)آ² آ· \|kâں© = (N(N+2)/4) آ· \|kâں©`. Direct consequence of `(إœ)آ² = (N(N+2)/4) آ· 1` (خ²-14) â€” every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (خ²-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `إœ^{(خ±)}` and `إœ^آ±` commutes with the Casimir `(إœ)آ² = (N(N+2)/4) آ· 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (خ²-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[إœ^+, إœ^-] = 2 آ· إœ^{(3)}`. Combines خ²-12 (`إœ^+ آ· إœ^- = diag((i + 1)(N âˆ’ i))`) and خ²-13 (`إœ^- آ· إœ^+ = diag(i (N âˆ’ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) âˆ’ i(N-i+1) = N âˆ’ 2i = 2(N/2 âˆ’ i)` matches `2 آ· إœ^{(3)}` (خ²-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[إœ^{(1)}, إœ^{(2)}] = i آ· إœ^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `إœ^{(1)} = (إœ^+ + إœ^-)/2` and `إœ^{(2)} = (إœ^+ âˆ’ إœ^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) âˆ’ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 إœ^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (خ²-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[إœ^{(2)}, إœ^{(3)}] = i آ· إœ^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan خ²-2 (`[إœ^{(3)}, إœ^آ±] = آ±إœ^آ±`) via `إœ^{(2)} = (إœ^+ âˆ’ إœ^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-إœ^+ âˆ’ إœ^-) = (-1/(2i)) آ· 2 إœ^{(1)} = i آ· إœ^{(1)}` (خ²-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[إœ^{(3)}, إœ^{(1)}] = i آ· إœ^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan خ²-2 via `إœ^{(1)} = (إœ^+ + إœ^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (إœ^+ âˆ’ إœ^-) = (1/2) آ· (2i) آ· إœ^{(2)} = i آ· إœ^{(2)}`. Together with خ²-20 and خ²-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (خ²-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kâں©âں¨k|`: `P_k آ· P_k = P_k` and `P_i آ· P_j = 0` for `i â‰  j`. Combined with خ²-9 (`âˆ‘_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `إœ^{(3)}` (خ²-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (خ²-25+) â€” lets us pull a polynomial in `إœ^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (خ²-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (إœ^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = âˆڈ_{j â‰  k} (إœ^{(3)} âˆ’ خ»_j â€¢ 1)/(خ»_k âˆ’ خ»_j)` with `خ»_j = (N : â„‚)/2 âˆ’ j.val`. Combines خ²-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `إœ^{(3)}`**, which combined with خ²-9 (`âˆ‘ P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1ج‚` (خ²-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `إœ^+ = إœ^{(1)} + i آ· إœ^{(2)}` and `إœ^- = إœ^{(1)} âˆ’ i آ· إœ^{(2)}`. The defining identities `إœ^{(1)} = (1/2)(إœ^+ + إœ^-)`, `إœ^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(إœ^+ âˆ’ إœ^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (خ²-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k âˆˆ Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k âˆˆ Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(1)}, إœ^{(2)}, إœ^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (خ²-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (خ²-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(خ±)}}`**: `إœ^+, إœ^- âˆˆ Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(1)}, إœ^{(2)}, إœ^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of خ²-26 (`إœ^آ± = إœ^{(1)} آ± i آ· إœ^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `â„‚`-linear combinations (خ²-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(خ±)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} âˆˆ Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(1)}, إœ^{(2)}, إœ^{(3)}}`. Combines خ²-10 (`إœ^+ آ· P_{i+1} = âˆڑ((i+1)(N-i)) آ· E_{i, i+1}`) with خ²-27 (`P_k âˆˆ adjoin`) and خ²-28 (`إœ^آ± âˆˆ adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{إœ^{(خ±)}}` (خ²-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(خ±)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} âˆˆ Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(خ±)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is خ²-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} آ· E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (خ²-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(خ±)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to خ²-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is خ²-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is خ²-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (خ²-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **ًںژ¯ Tasaki آ§2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `â„‚^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1ج‚, إœ^{(1)}, إœ^{(2)}, إœ^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin â„‚ {إœ^{(1)}, إœ^{(2)}, إœ^{(3)}} = âٹ¤`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} âˆˆ adjoin` (خ²-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = âˆ‘_{i,j} M_{i,j} â€¢ E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (خ²-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `دƒ : خ› â†’ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `إœ_i^{(خ±)}, إœ_i^آ±`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki آ§2.5 Phase B-خ² خ²-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSiteCore.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

### Basis states and raising/lowering (Tasaki آ§2.1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, آ§2.1 eqs. (2.1.4), (2.1.5), (2.1.6), p. 14.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinHalfUp`, `spinHalfDown` | `\|دˆ^â†‘âں©`, `\|دˆ^â†“âں©` as column vectors (Tasaki (2.1.6)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, آ§2.3 eqs. (2.3.4)â€“(2.3.8), pp. 26â€“27.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `timeReversalSpinHalf` | `خکج‚(v) = (-conj(v 1), conj(v 0))` (Tasaki eq. (2.3.6), `S = 1/2`); the antilinear unitary `أ»_2 آ· Kج‚` of آ§2.3 | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfUp` / `_spinHalfDown` | `خکج‚|دˆ^â†‘âں© = |دˆ^â†“âں©` and `خکج‚|دˆ^â†“âں© = -|دˆ^â†‘âں©` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_add` | additivity `خکج‚(v + w) = خکج‚(v) + خکج‚(w)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_smul` | **antilinearity** in the scalar: `خکج‚(c â€¢ v) = (conj c) â€¢ خکج‚(v)` (Tasaki آ§2.3 antilinearity warning, pp. 26â€“27) | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_sq` | **Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki eq. (2.3.8), half-odd-integer): `خکج‚آ² = -1ج‚` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | spin sign flip (Tasaki eq. (2.3.14)): `خکج‚(إœ^(خ±) آ· v) = (-إœ^(خ±)) آ· (خکج‚ v)` for `خ± = 1, 2, 3` â€” equivariance form of `خکج‚ إœ^(خ±) خکج‚âپ»آ¹ = -إœ^(خ±)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf` | the antilinear complex-conjugation map `Kج‚` of Tasaki آ§2.3 eq. (2.3.4) at `S = 1/2`: `Kج‚(v) i := conj(v i)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf_sq` / `_add` / `_smul` | `Kج‚` is involutive (`Kج‚آ² = id`), additive, and antilinear in the scalar | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_eq_spinHalfRot2_pi_mulVec` | the **factorisation identity** of Tasaki آ§2.3: `خکج‚ = أ»_2 آ· Kج‚` where `أ»_2 = spinHalfRot2 د€` is the د€ rotation about the `2`-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `flipConfig` / `flipConfig_apply` / `flipConfig_involutive` | the spin-flip on a many-body configuration `دƒ : خ› â†’ Fin 2`: `flipConfig دƒ x := 1 - دƒ x`; involutive | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign` / `timeReversalSign_zero` / `_one` / `_mul_flip` | per-site sign factor `خµ(0) = 1`, `خµ(1) = -1` for the multi-spin time-reversal, with `خµ(s) آ· خµ(1 - s) = -1` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` | multi-spin time-reversal map (Tasaki آ§2.3 lattice extension, `S = 1/2`): `(خکج‚_tot v) د„ := (âˆڈ_x خµ(د„ x)) آ· conj(v (flip د„))` for finite `خ›` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_conj` / `timeReversalSign_prod_mul_flip` | the product `âˆڈ_x خµ(د„ x)` is real (conjugation invariant); the cross product equals `(-1)^|خ›|` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_sq` | **Multi-spin Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki آ§2.3 half-odd-integer extension): `خکج‚_totآ² = (-1)^|خ›| آ· 1ج‚` â€” `+1ج‚` when `|خ›|` is even, `-1ج‚` when odd | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec` | action of `خکج‚_tot` on a many-body basis state: `خکج‚_tot |خ¨_دƒâں© = (âˆڈ_x خµ(flip دƒ x)) آ· |خ¨_{flip دƒ}âں©` â€” natural many-body generalisation of `خکج‚|â†‘âں© = |â†“âں©` and `خکج‚|â†“âں© = -|â†‘âں©` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x دƒ^x).mulVec |خ¨_دƒâں© = |خ¨_{siteFlipAt دƒ x}âں©` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x دƒ^x).mulVec v) د„ = v (siteFlipAt د„ x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `âˆڈ_y خµ((siteFlipAt د„ x) y) = -(âˆڈ_y خµ(د„ y))` â€” the per-site flip swaps `خµ(د„ x)` with `خµ(1 - د„ x) = -خµ(د„ x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `دƒ^x` (Tasaki آ§2.3 (2.3.14) at خ± = 1): `خکج‚_tot ((onSite x دƒ^x) v) = (-(onSite x دƒ^x))(خکج‚_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `دƒ^y` (Tasaki آ§2.3 (2.3.14) at خ± = 2): `خکج‚_tot ((onSite x دƒ^y) v) = (-(onSite x دƒ^y))(خکج‚_tot v)`. | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `خکج‚_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`خکج‚_tot(c â€¢ v) = conj(c) â€¢ خکج‚_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) â€” foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki آ§2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `إœ^(خ±) = دƒ^(خ±) / 2`: `خکج‚_tot ((onSite x إœ^(خ±)) v) = (-(onSite x إœ^(خ±)))(خکج‚_tot v)` for خ± = 1, 2, 3 â€” direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `إœ_x آ· إœ_y`** (Tasaki آ§2.3): `خکج‚_tot ((إœ_x آ· إœ_y) v) = (إœ_x آ· إœ_y)(خکج‚_tot v)` â€” two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki آ§2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `خکج‚_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(خکج‚_tot v)`. | `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : â„‌` makes `(-(J : â„‚))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `خکج‚_tot` for any real coupling `J : â„‌`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `خکج‚_tot |â†‘â†“âں© = -|â†“â†‘âں©` and `خکج‚_tot |â†“â†‘âں© = -|â†‘â†“âں©` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|â†‘â†“âں© - |â†“â†‘âں©` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki آ§2.3 / آ§A.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `خکج‚_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|â†‘â†“âں© + |â†“â†‘âں©` is **anti-invariant** under `خکج‚_tot`: `خکج‚_tot (|â†‘â†“âں© + |â†“â†‘âں©) = -(|â†‘â†“âں© + |â†“â†‘âں©)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, آ§2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `خ›` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `خ› = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp خ›` | `Matrix (خ› â†’ Fin 2) (خ› â†’ Fin 2) â„‚` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : خ›` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian â†’ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x â‰  y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A آ· onSite i B = onSite i (A آ· B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `دˆ` also fixes `دˆ` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(Aآ·B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the Jordanâ€“Wigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### Horschâ€“von der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki آ§3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3â€“10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki آ§3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)â€“(3.4.12), pp. 66â€“67.

The proof collapses `Tr(خ¸(A)آ·A) = âˆ‘_{دƒ,خ¼} خ¸(A)دƒخ¼آ·Aخ¼دƒ` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `خ¼=دƒ`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A دƒ دƒ` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(خ¸(A)آ·A) = conj S آ· S = â€–Sâ€–آ² â‰¥ 0` with `S = âˆ‘_â„“ D â„“`. | `xyHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_5_1_xy_odlro_half_filling` | **Theorem 5.1** (آ§5.1â€“آ§5.2, AXIOM; eqs. (5.1.1)â€“(5.2.5)): **off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO) of hard-core bosons at half filling**. Hard-core bosons (`uâ†‘âˆ‍` limit of the bosonic Hubbard model, eq. 5.1.4) are **equivalent to the S=1/2 XY model** (eq. 5.1.5) via the identification `أ¢_xâ€ â†”(âˆ’1)^x إœ_x^+`, `nج‚_xâ†”إœ_x^(3)+آ½`, `Nج‚â†”إœ_tot^(3)+L^d/2` (eqs. 5.1.6â€“5.1.7); half filling `دپ=آ½` â†” the `إœ_tot^(3)=0` sector. `xyHamiltonianS d L` = the XY Hamiltonian (XXZ at خ»=D=0, N=1). The bosonic order operators `أ”_L^(1,2)` (eqs. 5.2.2/5.2.4) are the staggered XY-plane spin order operators. For `d â‰¥ 2` (`hd : 2 â‰¤ d`) and half filling, ODLRO holds with `qâ‚€ > 0` (depending only on `d`): every ground state `خ¦_GS` (eigenvector + min-energy, `خ¦â‰ 0`, `إœ_tot^(3) خ¦=0`) of `xyHamiltonianS` satisfies `âں¨(أ”_L^(خ±))آ²âں©/âں¨خ¦,خ¦âں©/(L^d)آ² â‰¥ qâ‚€` for خ±=1,2 (`خ±:Fin 3, خ±â‰ 2`) and all large even `L` (eq. 5.2.5; the squared operator `أ”_L^(خ±)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)آ²` denominator, the intensive ODLRO density). Half filling is essential; the ground state shows LRO **without** SSB (eq. 5.2.8). Proof: reflection positivity (Kennedyâ€“Liebâ€“Shastry, Kuboâ€“Kishi) | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |
| `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS` / `IsBECTowerConstants` / `tasaki_5_2_bec_tower` | **Theorem 5.2** (آ§5.3, AXIOM; eqs. (5.3.2)â€“(5.3.4)): **low-lying tower states of hard-core bosons**. The phase-`خ¸` **coherent state** `خ‍_خ¸ = (2M_max+1)^{âˆ’1/2} خ£_{M=âˆ’M_max}^{M_max} e^{âˆ’iMخ¸} خ“_M` (eq. 5.3.5; `خ“_M` = normalized tower state), with a slow window `M_max(L)` (`IsSlowBECWindow`: monotone, â†’ âˆ‍, `â‰¤ Câ‚پL^{d/2}` eventually). | `CoupledSite` / `coupledHamiltonian` / `coupledCrossCorrelation` / `tasaki_5_4_coupled_bec_ssb` | **Theorem 5.4** (آ§5.5, AXIOM; eqs. (5.5.1)â€“(5.5.6)): **symmetry breaking in coupled BEC**. The coupled two-species lattice `CoupledSite = HypercubicTorus أ— Bool` (`(x,false)=a`, `(x,true)=b`); total Hamiltonian `ؤ¤_tot^خµ = ؤ¤_a + ؤ¤_b + خµ ؤ¤_tunnel` (`coupledHamiltonian`, eq. 5.5.1): the two uncoupled boson copies `ؤ¤_a+ؤ¤_b = 2(ؤ¤_XY,a+ؤ¤_XY,b)` (the boson factor `ؤ¤=2ؤ¤_XY`, consistent with `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS`, so `خµ` is the textbook tunneling strength; via `sameSpeciesNNCoupling`) + the tunneling term `tunnelHamiltonian د† = âˆ’خ£_x (e^{iد†} إœ^+_a إœ^âˆ’_b + e^{âˆ’iد†} إœ^âˆ’_a إœ^+_b)` (eq. 5.5.3). Total `2N` particles = doubled half filling `إœ_tot^(3)=0`. Assuming single-system ODLRO (`hODLRO`: the uncoupled XY ground states satisfy the Theorem 5.1 bound with `qâ‚€`, tying `qâ‚€` to genuine ODLRO), the unique coupled ground state `خ¦^خµ` develops a definite relative U(1) phase: âˆƒ `mجƒ â‰¥ âˆڑ(2qâ‚€)` with `lim_{خµâ†“0} lim_{Lâ†‘âˆ‍} âں¨خ¦^خµ, أ¢â€ _{(x,a)}أ¢_{(x,b)} خ¦^خµâں©/âں¨خ¦^خµ,خ¦^خµâں© = mجƒآ²e^{âˆ’iد†}` (eq. 5.5.5; `coupledCrossCorrelation = إœ^+_{(x,a)}إœ^âˆ’_{(x,b)}`) **and** the conjugate `âں¨خ¦^خµ, أ¢_{(x,a)}أ¢â€ _{(x,b)} خ¦^خµâں©/âں¨آ·âں© = mجƒآ²e^{+iد†}` (eq. 5.5.6; `coupledCrossCorrelationConj = إœ^âˆ’_{(x,a)}إœ^+_{(x,b)}`). Double limit in eventual-`خµ'` form (outer `خµâ†“0`, inner `Lâ†‘âˆ‍`); GS family given (unique by MLM). The two condensates are coherently coupled (entangled). Proof: Komaâ€“Tasaki. **Completes Tasaki Chapter 5** | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `آ±1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`Mâ‚€ = خکآ·M_realâ‚€آ·خک`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` â€” transferring the **full Perronâ€“Frobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `خکv`, ground minimality, and `finrank â‰¤ 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `â„‚` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` â€” a single-`آ±` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is amplitude `|âں¨دƒ'|إœ_xآ·إœ_y|دƒâں©| â‰¤ 1` (each `âˆڑ` factor `â‰¤ âˆڑ2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` â€” for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M دƒ د„| â‰¤ (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `آ±1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym آ· spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `â‰¤ 1`). **Magnetization-`آ±1` sector min `â‰¥ âˆ’2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`آ±` `Sz=آ±1` (`pmCount = 1`) â€” there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector Liebâ€“Mattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `آ±` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `د„` to be the slide `slidePM` of `دƒ`'s `آ±` spin to its new site, sharing the `آ±` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `â‰¤ ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `آ±`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`âˆ‘_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` âں¹ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `â‰¤ 2`: every single-`آ±` eigenvalue `â‰¥ âˆ’2`) â€” the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = آ±1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`â‰¤ âˆ’L â‰¤ âˆ’2`). **Balanced ground energy `< âˆ’L` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the Perronâ€“Frobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the Nأ©el row gives `خ¼آ·v(Nأ©el) = âˆ’Lآ·v(Nأ©el) + خ£_{د„â‰ Nأ©el} Mâ‚€(Nأ©el,د„) v(د„)`; every off-diagonal term is `â‰¤ 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`آ±` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`Mâ‚€(Nأ©el,Nأ©el) = âˆ’L`) we get `خ¼آ·v(Nأ©el) < âˆ’Lآ·v(Nأ©el)`, hence `خ¼ < âˆ’L`. Since `âˆ’L â‰¤ âˆ’2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`آ±1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** â€” the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` â€” an odd count `â‰¥ 3` is impossible. Proof: the `آ±` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` â€” no `آ±` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `آ±` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `آ±` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - آ·` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `آ±` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` â€” the cyclic wrap arc holds no `آ±` spin), which would force the first and last `آ±` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count â€” contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount âں¹ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`آ±` âں¹ `magSumS = L آ± 1 â‰  L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`آ±` `= Sz آ±1`, with no higher sectors â€” the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`آ±` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish â€” magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{دƒ // P دƒ}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`آ±` config couples only to single-`آ±`, an even config only to even) â€” the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`آ±` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank â‰¤ 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `â„‚` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` â€” the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (realâ†’complex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced Perronâ€“Frobenius `finrank â‰¤ 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` â€” the **complex single-`آ±` block has all eigenvalues `â‰¥ âˆ’2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `â‰¥ âˆ’2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < âˆ’2` the single-`آ±` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) â€” the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced Perronâ€“Frobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The Perronâ€“Frobenius uniqueness development â€” kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`آ±1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy â€” lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

(8.2.1)â€“(8.2.4)): the two-parameter Haldane-phase model. `lambdaDChainHamiltonianS L خ» D = خ£_{x,y} [openAnisotropicChainCoupling] آ· spinSDotXXZ x y خ» 2 + D خ£_x (إœ_x^{(3)})آ²` (eq. 8.2.1, `ؤ¤_{خ»,D}` = open XXZ chain with Ising anisotropy `خ»` + crystal field `D`; `خ»=1` â‡’ open-boundary version of the anisotropic model 8.1.1, agreeing in the bulk). `neelOrderParameterS`/`stringOrderParameterS` (markers, `â„‌â†’â„‌â†’Fin 3â†’â„‌`) are the thermodynamic double-limit Nأ©el (8.2.2) and den Nijsâ€“Rommelse string (8.2.3) order parameters of the GS. `tasaki_theorem_8_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.2.4): for `خ»â‰¥0` and any `D`, `O_Nأ©el^{(خ±)} â‰¤ O_string^{(خ±)}` for all `خ±` â€” nonzero Nأ©el order (Ising AF phase) forces nonzero hidden order. `tasaki_neel_transverse_eq_nonneg` (`خ»â‰¥0`): `O_Nأ©el^{(1)} = O_Nأ©el^{(2)} â‰¥ 0`. | `ktUnitaryS` / `piRotationS` / `IsZ2Z2Invariant` / `tasaki_prop_8_4` | **آ§8.2.2â€“آ§8.2.3 Kennedyâ€“Tasaki transformation + Proposition 8.4** (Pollmannâ€“Turnerâ€“Bergâ€“Oshikawa; eqs. (8.2.5)â€“(8.2.7)): the nonlocal unitary realizing hidden Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚ symmetry breaking. `ktUnitaryS L` (marker) is the Kennedyâ€“Tasaki unitary `أ›_KT = âˆڈ_{u<v} exp(iد€ إœ_u^{(3)} إœ_v^{(1)})` (eq. 8.2.5), with `ktUnitaryS_sq` (`أ›_KTآ²=1`) and `ktUnitaryS_selfAdjoint` (`أ›_KT=أ›_KTâ€ `) â€” a self-adjoint involution. `piRotationS L خ± = âˆڈ_x exp(iد€ إœ_x^{(خ±)})` (**concrete**, on-site matrix exponentials) is the د€-rotation about axis `خ±`; `IsZ2Z2Invariant H` = `(أ›_د€^{(خ±)})â€  H أ›_د€^{(خ±)} = H` for all `خ±` (commutes with all three د€-rotations). `HasShortRangeInteraction`/`HasSomeShortRangeInteraction` (markers) capture range-`r` locality. `tasaki_prop_8_4` (**AXIOM**): for a short-range open-chain `ؤ¤`, `أ›_KT ؤ¤ أ›_KT` is again short-range **iff** `ؤ¤` is Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚ invariant â€” the hidden-symmetry-breaking picture is effective exactly when `ؤ¤` has Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚ symmetry | `Quantum/SpinS/KennedyTasakiTransformation.lean` |
| `guWenHamiltonianS` / `IsSPTPhase` / `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` | **آ§8.3.1â€“آ§8.3.2 Symmetry protected topological (SPT) phase** (Guâ€“Wen / Pollmannâ€“Turnerâ€“Bergâ€“Oshikawa; eqs. (8.3.1)â€“(8.3.4)): the Haldane phase as an SPT phase. `guWenHamiltonianS L D B = anisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D + B خ£_x إœ_x^{(1)}` (eq. 8.3.4, S=1 chain + magnetic field B in 1-dir, breaks Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚â†’Zâ‚‚; B=0 â‡’ anisotropic 8.1.1). `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.3.3): the spin-`S` VBS string order `O_string^{(خ±)}` is `>0` for odd `S`, `=0` for even `S` (even/odd-`S` distinction); `tasaki_vbs_edge_degeneracy` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` AKLT open chain has `(S+1)آ²`-fold edge degeneracy. **SPT definition** (`def : Prop`, NOT axiom): `HamiltonianPath` (continuous `â„‌â†’` Hamiltonian, each short-range gapped-unique via `IsShortRangeGappedUniqueGS` marker); `ContinuouslyConnected`/`SymmetryConnected sym` (path with all `ؤ¤_s` having the symmetry `sym`); `IsTrivialPhase sym H` = symmetry-connected to a product state (`IsProductStateHamiltonian`); `IsSPTPhase sym H` = short-range gapped-unique + `sym H` + آ¬trivial. The Haldane phase is the prototypical SPT phase protected by Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhase.lean` |
| `IsTimeReversalInvariant` / `IsBondInversionInvariant` / `vbsInversionParityS` / `tasaki_spt_classification` | **آ§8.3.2â€“آ§8.3.3 Protecting symmetries + topological indices for SPT** (Pollmannâ€“Turnerâ€“Bergâ€“Oshikawa; eqs. (8.3.6)â€“(8.3.10)): the Haldane phase is protected by any of three symmetries â€” (S1) Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`), (S2) time-reversal (`IsTimeReversalInvariant` marker), (S3) bond-centered inversion (`IsBondInversionInvariant` marker). `vbsInversionParityS L S` (marker, â„¤) + `tasaki_vbs_inversion_parity` (**AXIOM**): `أ›_inv|خ¦_VBS^Sâں© = (âˆ’1)^{Lآ·S}|خ¦_VBS^Sâں©` â€” odd `Lآ·S` âں¹ odd parity âں¹ Zâ‚‚ obstruction to connecting to the trivial state. `IsSpinSVBSNontrivialSPT S` (marker) + `tasaki_spt_classification` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` VBS is a nontrivial SPT phase **iff `S` is odd** (even `S` â‡’ trivial). `entanglementEntropyS` (marker, eqs. 8.3.7â€“8.3.8): the bipartite entanglement entropy `âˆ’خ£ p_j log p_j` from the Schmidt decomposition. آ§8.3.3 is heuristic; precise indices come in آ§8.3.4 (MPS) / آ§8.3.6 (Ogata) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTTopologicalIndex.lean` |
| `IsTrivialProjectiveRep` / `tasaki_theorem_8_7` / `tasaki_corollary_8_5` | **آ§8.3.4 Matrix-product SPT index** (Theorem 8.7 Tachikawa + Corollary 8.5; eqs. (8.3.42)â€“(8.3.47)): the precise MPS invariant. A protecting symmetry `G` acts on the bond space by a projective representation with phase function (2-cocycle) `د† : Gâ†’Gâ†’â„‌` (`IsProjectiveRep` marker); it is **trivial** (`IsTrivialProjectiveRep` marker) iff `د†` is a coboundary (eq. 8.3.43) â€” the cohomology class is the SPT index. `SymmetricInjectiveMPSExists G د†` (marker): an injective MPS invariant up to phase under `Vج‚(g)`. `tasaki_theorem_8_7` (**AXIOM**): symmetric injective MPS âں¹ trivial projective rep. For half-odd-integer spin (`N` odd), `z2z2Spin_nontrivial_of_odd` (**AXIOM**, eq. 2.1.31): the Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚ rep is nontrivial. `tasaki_corollary_8_5` (**PROVED**, contrapositive of Thm 8.7): for `N` odd there is **no** Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚-invariant injective MPS â€” the matrix-product Liebâ€“Schultzâ€“Mattis no-go | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTMatrixProductIndex.lean` |
| `tasaki_theorem_8_6` | **آ§8.3.5 Discrete-symmetry Liebâ€“Schultzâ€“Mattis theorem** (Theorem 8.6, Ogataâ€“Tasaki): the general no-go without continuous symmetry. For **half-odd-integer** spin (`S=N/2`, `N` odd) with a short-ranged translation-invariant Hamiltonian (`HasShortRangeHamiltonianS`/`IsTranslationInvariantS` markers) having **either** Zâ‚‚أ—Zâ‚‚ **or** time-reversal symmetry (`IsZ2Z2SymmetricS âˆ¨ IsTimeReversalSymmetricS`), the ground state can **never** be both unique and gapped: `tasaki_theorem_8_6` (**AXIOM**) `âں¹ آ¬HasUniqueGappedGroundStateS H`. Unlike the original LSM (آ§6.2, continuous U(1)), this needs only a discrete symmetry; proved rigorously by Ogataâ€“Tasaki via the Cuntz algebra / split property | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisDiscrete.lean` |
| `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_8` | **آ§8.3.6 Rigorous SPT phase transition** (Theorem 8.8, Tasaki 2018 index theorem; eq. (8.3.55)): the existence of a topological phase transition. `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS L s = sآ·akltHamiltonianS L + (1âˆ’s)آ·خ£_x (إœ_x^{(3)})آ²` (eq. 8.3.55) linearly connects the trivial Hamiltonian (8.1.2, `s=0`, large-`D` phase) to the AKLT/VBS Hamiltonian (7.1.1, `s=1`, Haldane phase), keeping all three protecting symmetries. `tasaki_theorem_8_8` (**AXIOM**): the model's infinite-volume limit undergoes a phase transition â€” âˆƒ intermediate `sâˆˆ(0,1)` at which the family `Lâ†¦ؤ¤_s` is **either** gapless (`IsGaplessInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has multiple ground states (`HasMultipleGroundStatesInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has a local-expectation discontinuity (`HasLocalExpectationDiscontinuityInInfiniteVolume`) â€” markers on the `Lâ†‘âˆ‍` family. Proving topological phase transitions resists standard methods; Tasaki settled it via an index theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhaseTransition.lean` |
| `toricCodeHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_9` | **آ§8.4 Topological order: Kitaev's toric code** (Theorem 8.9, Bravyiâ€“Hastingsâ€“Michalakis; eqs. (8.4.1), (8.4.19)): a gapped phase whose degeneracy is protected by topology, not symmetry. Qubits live on the edges `ToricEdge L = (ZMod L أ— ZMod L) أ— Fin 2` of an `Lأ—L` torus (`2Lآ²` edges); `starEdges`/`plaquetteEdges` are the 4-edge stars `A_v`/plaquettes `B_p`; `sigmaZS`/`sigmaXS` = `دƒ^z`/`دƒ^x`. `toricCodeHamiltonianS L = âˆ’خ£_v âˆڈ_{A_v} دƒ^z âˆ’ خ£_p âˆڈ_{B_p} دƒ^x` (eq. 8.4.1, commuting stabilizers, gap â‰¥ 2, 4-fold degenerate on the torus). `perturbedToricHamiltonianS L خµ V = ؤ¤_tc + خµ خ£_x V_x` (eq. 8.4.19). `tasaki_theorem_8_9` (**AXIOM**): for range-`r` perturbations there are **L-independent** `خµâ‚€,خ”>0` such that for `|خµ|â‰¤خµâ‚€`, all `Lâ‰¥2` (nontrivial torus), and any local perturbation (`IsToricPerturbation`), the perturbed Hamiltonian has **four near-degenerate ground states** separated by `خ”` (`HasFourNearlyDegenerateGroundStates`) â€” topological 4-fold degeneracy stable under arbitrary perturbation, no symmetry needed. Lattice + stabilizers + both Hamiltonians concrete | `Quantum/SpinS/ToricCode.lean` |

Mirrors PR #2825 for the asymmetric form (PR #2843, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReal.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_nonpos` | **non-positivity of the predicted symmetric min energy**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re â‰¤ 0`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_neg_of_nondegenerate` | **strict negativity of the predicted symmetric min energy** (non-degenerate case): `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re < 0` when both `\|A\| â‰¥ 1`, `\|آ¬A\| â‰¥ 1`, and `N â‰¥ 1`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_eq` | **norm of the predicted symmetric min energy**: `â€–bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A Nâ€– = \|A\|آ·\|آ¬A\|آ·Nآ²/2 + min(\|A\|, \|آ¬A\|)آ·N`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_eq_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **symmetric predicted min energy vanishes at saturated edges**: at `\|آ¬A\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`, `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N = 0`. Specialisation `B = A` of `sublatticeSpinSDot_eq_sum_sum` (PR #1054), foundation for sublattice Casimir eigenvalue formulas on constant-on-A configurations (خ³-4 step 17) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` (PR #1058) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **spin-`S` sublattice Casimir eigenvalue on the all-up state**: `(إœ_A)آ² آ· \|دƒ_âٹ¤âں© = ((\|A\|آ·N/2)آ·(\|A\|آ·N/2+1)) آ· \|دƒ_âٹ¤âں©` â€” the maximum-spin Casimir value of the `A`-subsystem at total spin `J_A = \|A\|آ·N/2 = \|A\|آ·S`. Companion to the existing diagonal `_inner_self` (= 1) lemma; together they witness that the computational basis is orthonormal (خ³-4 step 210) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1257) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_basisVec` | **Combined خ´-form**: `<basisVecS د„ \| basisVecS دƒ> = if د„ = دƒ then 1 else 0` (and spin-`1/2` analogue). Single-statement combination of the diagonal-1 and off-diagonal-0 facts (خ³-4 step 211) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1258) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_totalSpinSOp3_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_totalSpinHalfOp3_basisVec` | **Combined `إœ_tot^(3)` خ´-form matrix element**: `<basisVecS د„ \| إœ_tot^(3) \| basisVecS دƒ> = if د„ = دƒ then magEigenvalueS دƒ else 0` (spin-`S`); spin-`1/2` analogue with `M(دƒ)/2`. Single-statement combination of خ³-4 step 206/207 (diagonal) and خ³-4 step 209 (off-diagonal) (خ³-4 step 212) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1259) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_totalSpinSOp3_sq_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_basisVec` | **Combined `(إœ_tot^(3))آ²` خ´-form matrix element**: `<basisVecS د„ \| (إœ_tot^(3))آ² \| basisVecS دƒ> = if د„ = دƒ then (magEigenvalueS دƒ)آ² else 0` (spin-`S`); spin-`1/2` analogue with `(M(دƒ)/2)آ²`. Off-diagonal proven via the `إœ_tot^(3)` eigenvector identity applied twice + basis orthogonality (خ³-4 step 213) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1260) |
| `basisVecS_expectation_totalSpinSOp1` / `_totalSpinSOp2` | **Spin-`S` transverse `إœ_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVecS دƒ \| إœ_tot^(1) \| basisVecS دƒ> = 0` and `<basisVecS دƒ \| إœ_tot^(2) \| basisVecS دƒ> = 0`. Direct from per-site `basisVecS_expectation_onSiteS_spinSOp{1,2}` summed over sites via `Matrix.sum_mulVec` + `dotProduct_sum` (خ³-4 step 214) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleSiteTransverseMeanZero.lean` (PR #1261) |
| `basisVec_expectation_totalSpinHalfOp1` / `_totalSpinHalfOp2` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse `إœ_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVec دƒ \| إœ_tot^(1) \| basisVec دƒ> = 0` and `<basisVec دƒ \| إœ_tot^(2) \| basisVec دƒ> = 0`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of خ³-4 step 214; per-site uses `basisVec_expectation_eq_diagonal` + `pauliX/Y` zero diagonal (خ³-4 step 215) | `Quantum/SpinDot/Hamiltonian.lean` (PR #1262) |
| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` `(إœ_tot)آ²` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVec دƒ`**: `<basisVec دƒ \| (إœ_tot)آ² \| basisVec دƒ> = \|خ›\|/2 + (M(دƒ)/2)آ²` for any `دƒ : خ› â†’ Fin 2`. Generalises خ³-4 step 201 (constant case has `M = آ±\|خ›\|` giving `\|خ›\|آ·(\|خ›\|+2)/4`). Proof: reduce to diagonal matrix element, expand `(إœ_tot)آ² = خ£_{x,y} spinHalfDot x y`, unify per-pair diagonal as `m_x m_y/4 + (1/2)آ·[x=y]`, then split via separability (خ³-4 step 216) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1263) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` `(إœ_tot)آ²` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVecS دƒ`**: `<basisVecS دƒ \| (إœ_tot)آ² \| basisVecS دƒ> = \|V\|آ·N(N+2)/4 + magEigenvalueS(دƒ)آ² âˆ’ خ£_x (N/2 âˆ’ دƒx.val)آ²` for any `دƒ : خ› â†’ Fin (N + 1)`. Spin-`S` analogue of خ³-4 step 216; the per-site sum-of-squares term is the configuration-dependent z-axis squared contribution that doesn't collapse for spin-`S`. Spin-`1/2` is the special case where the term reduces to constant `\|خ›\|/4` (خ³-4 step 218) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1265) |
| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir `(إœ_tot^(1))آ² + (إœ_tot^(2))آ²` on arbitrary `basisVec دƒ` is constant `\|خ›\|/2`**: independent of `دƒ`. | `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(إœ_tot^(1))آ² + (إœ_tot^(2))آ²` on arbitrary `basisVecS دƒ` = `\|V\|آ·N(N+2)/4 âˆ’ خ£_x (N/2 âˆ’ دƒx.val)آ²`**. | `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization خ› (neelConfigOf (آ¬A)) = âˆ’magnetization خ› (neelConfigOf A)`. The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = خ£_{j=1}^z إœ_0 آ· إœ_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `âˆ’S(1 + zS)` (خ³-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j âˆˆ univ.erase 0` (خ³-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = âˆ…` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `âˆ’S(1 + zS)` is intended for `z â‰¥ 1` (خ³-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<خ¦_âٹ¤\|H\|خ¦_âٹ¤> = zآ·(N/2)آ²` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `âˆ’S(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (خ³-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<خ¦_aligned(c)\|H\|خ¦_aligned(c)> = zآ·(N/2 âˆ’ c.val)آ²`. Generalises خ³-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `zآ·(N/2)آ²` (خ³-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<خ¦_âٹ¥\|H\|خ¦_âٹ¥> = zآ·(N/2)آ²`. Direct specialisation of خ³-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (خ³-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `إœ_R^(خ±) = خ£_{j â‰  0} onSiteS j إœ^(خ±)` for axes `خ± = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `إœ^(خ±)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (إœ_totآ² âˆ’ إœ_0آ² âˆ’ إœ_Rآ²)/2` (خ³-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`إœ_0 آ· إœ_R` decomposition**: `H = خ£_خ± onSiteS 0 (إœ^(خ±)) آ· leafSpinSOp_خ±`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `خ£_j (A * B_j) = A * (خ£_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (خ³-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_خ± (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (إœ^(خ±)) + leafSpinSOp_خ± z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (خ³-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `إœ_Rآ² := (إœ_R^(1))آ² + (إœ_R^(2))آ² + (إœ_R^(3))آ²`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (خ³-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 إœ^(خ±)) (leafSpinSOp_خ± z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (خ³-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2آ·H = (إœ_tot)آ² âˆ’ إœ_0آ² âˆ’ إœ_Rآ²` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `إœ_0آ² = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `إœ_Rآ² = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `خ£_خ± totalSpinSOp_خ±آ² = خ£_خ± (onSite 0 + leaf_خ±)آ²` via خ³-5 step 251 + `(a+b)آ² = aآ² + 2ab + bآ²` (using خ³-5 step 253 commutativity) + خ³-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (خ³-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **â„‚-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : â„‚) â€¢ H = (إœ_tot)آ² âˆ’ إœ_0آ² âˆ’ إœ_Rآ²`. Direct corollary of خ³-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (خ³-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 آ· <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stotآ²\|v> âˆ’ <v\|S0آ²\|v> âˆ’ <v\|SRآ²\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of خ³-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (خ³-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1303) |

| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_simplified` | explicit eigenvalue of `ؤ¤_toy` on the all-aligned state: the Casimir-difference form `\|خ›\|آ·(\|خ›\|+2)/4 âˆ’ \|A\|آ·(\|A\|+2)/4 âˆ’ \|آ¬A\|آ·(\|آ¬A\|+2)/4` algebraically simplifies via `\|خ›\| = \|A\| + \|آ¬A\|` to the **product form** `\|A\|آ·\|آ¬A\|/2`. | `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_neelStateOf` / `_complement_mulVec_neelStateOf` | sublattice Casimir eigenvalues on the **Nأ©el state** `خ¦_Nأ©el(A) := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`: `(إœ_A)آ² آ· \|خ¦_Nأ©el(A)âں© = (\|A\|آ·(\|A\|+2)/4) آ· \|خ¦_Nأ©el(A)âں©` and `(إœ_آ¬A)آ² آ· \|خ¦_Nأ©el(A)âں© = (\|آ¬A\|آ·(\|آ¬A\|+2)/4) آ· \|خ¦_Nأ©el(A)âں©`. Direct corollaries of `_of_const_on` since the Nأ©el configuration is constant on each sublattice (`دƒ x = 0` on `A`, `دƒ x = 1` on `آ¬A`); the Nأ©el state is simultaneously a `(إœ_A)آ²` and `(إœ_آ¬A)آ²` eigenvector at maximum-spin eigenvalues | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelCore.lean` |
| `mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_commute_of_mem` | generic preservation: any operator `A` with `Commute A (إœtot^(3))` maps each magnetisation sector `H_M` to itself â€” operator-level form of Tasaki آ§2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 block-diagonalisation | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | Casimir specialisation: `إœtotآ²` preserves every `H_M` (since `[إœtotآ², إœtot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : خ› â†’ خ› â†’ â„‚` and `M : â„‚`, `v âˆˆ H_M` implies `H آ· v âˆˆ H_M` â€” the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki آ§2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, إœtot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : â„‚`, `v âˆˆ H_M` implies `إœtot^- آ· v âˆˆ H_{M-1}` â€” the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[إœtot^(3), إœtot^-] = -إœtot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : â„‚`, `v âˆˆ H_M` implies `إœtot^+ آ· v âˆˆ H_{M+1}` â€” the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[إœtot^(3), إœtot^+] = +إœtot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `خ› = Fin 2` and any `خ¸`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (أ›^(خ±)_خ¸) * onSite 1 (أ›^(خ±)_خ¸)` (general-خ¸ extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` â€” bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_eq_exp` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.11): `أ›^(خ±)_خ¸_tot = exp(-iخ¸ إœ_tot^(خ±))`. Composes `spinHalfRot{خ±}_eq_exp` (single site), `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` (per-site bridge), and `Matrix.exp_sum_of_commute` (commuting-summand exp = noncommProd of exps) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki آ§2.2 (2.2.12) â†’ (2.2.13): `Commute A (إœ_tot^(خ±)) â†’ Commute A (أ›^(خ±)_خ¸_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `أ›` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (إœ^آ±_tot) â†’ Commute A (exp(c â€¢ إœ^آ±_tot))` for any `c : â„‚` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(أ›^(خ±)_خ¸_tot)ل´´ * أ›^(خ±)_خ¸_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-iخ¸ إœ_tot^(خ±)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (إœ_tot^(خ±)) â†’ (أ›^(خ±)_خ¸_tot)ل´´ * A * أ›^(خ±)_خ¸_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

| `basisVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | `|دƒâں© âˆˆ H_{|دƒ|/2}` â€” basis states lie in their magnetization subspace | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_disjoint` | distinct sectors `H_M âٹ“ H_{M'} = âٹ¥` (`M â‰  M'`) â€” eigenvalue uniqueness | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body

| Lean name | Statement | File |
